note_data.title: Support and Movement
keyPoints: Support and movement are essential life processes — plants need support against gravity and wind, animals need movement for food and survival
Skeleton provides framework, protects internal organs, gives shape to body, and provides attachment sites for muscles
Hydrostatic skeleton found in earthworms uses body fluid pressure for support and movement
Exoskeleton in arthropods like insects and crustaceans provides external hard covering made of chitin
Endoskeleton in vertebrates is internal framework made of bones and cartilage
Human skeleton has 206 bones divided into axial skeleton (skull, vertebral column, rib cage) and appendicular skeleton (limbs)
Joints allow movement between bones — ball and socket joints allow movement in all directions, hinge joints allow movement in one plane only
Muscles work in antagonistic pairs — when one contracts, the other relaxes to produce movement
Tendons attach muscles to bones while ligaments connect bones to bones at joints

definitions.definition: The framework of hard or rigid parts that provides support and shape to the body of an organism
A type of skeleton found in soft-bodied animals where body fluids under pressure provide support and enable movement
An external hard covering that provides support and protection to the body, found in arthropods like insects and crustaceans
An internal framework of bones and cartilage that provides support to the body, found in vertebrates
The place where two or more bones meet and are held together by ligaments

importantQuestions.question: Differentiate between exoskeleton and endoskeleton.
importantQuestions.answer: Exoskeleton is external hard covering found in arthropods made of chitin, while endoskeleton is internal framework of bones and cartilage found in vertebrates. Exoskeleton provides protection but limits growth, endoskeleton allows continuous growth.

importantQuestions.question: Describe the structure and function of human skeletal system.
importantQuestions.answer: Human skeleton consists of 206 bones divided into axial and appendicular skeleton. Functions: (1) Provides framework and shape (2) Protects internal organs (3) Provides attachment for muscles (4) Produces blood cells in bone marrow (5) Stores calcium and phosphorus. Axial skeleton includes skull, vertebral column and rib cage. Appendicular skeleton includes arms and legs. Bones connected by joints allowing movement.

mcqs.question: How many bones are present in adult human skeleton?
Which type of skeleton is found in earthworm?
Ball and socket joint is found in:

commonMistakes: Students confuse exoskeleton with endoskeleton — remember exo means external, endo means internal
Mixing up tendons and ligaments — tendons connect muscle to bone, ligaments connect bone to bone
Forgetting to mention both axial and appendicular skeleton divisions in human skeleton questions
Not stating exact number of bones (206) in human adult skeleton
Confusing ball and socket joints with hinge joints — ball and socket allows movement in all directions
examTips: This chapter usually has 1-2 MCQs and 1 short question in every BSEK paper
Always memorize exact definitions as given in STBB textbook
Draw and label diagram of human skeleton — appears frequently in long questions
Remember examples: hydrostatic skeleton in earthworm, exoskeleton in cockroach, endoskeleton in humans
Functions of skeleton is a very common 5-mark question
note_data.keyDiagram: Human Skeleton Diagram showing axial skeleton (skull, vertebral column, rib cage) and appendicular skeleton (arms, legs) with proper labeling of major bones like femur, tibia, fibula, humerus, radius, ulna
pastPaperRefs: BSEK 2023 SSC-II Q3 (4 marks) — asked students to define skeletal system and list its functions
BSEK 2022 SSC-II Q7 (6 marks) — required comparison between cartilage and bone with examples
BSEK 2021 SSC-II Q4 (5 marks) — tested knowledge of muscle types and their characteristics
BSEK 2020 SSC-II Q6 (3 marks) — asked to define joints and classify them with one example each

workedExample.question: Define bone and cartilage. Compare any three characteristics of bone and cartilage. (6 marks)
steps: Step 1: Write exact textbook definition of bone
Step 2: Write exact textbook definition of cartilage
Step 3: Create comparison table with three characteristics
Step 4: Ensure all technical terms are spelled correctly
workedExample.answer: Bone: Bone is a hard connective tissue composed of calcium phosphate and collagen fibers that provides structural support to the body. Cartilage: Cartilage is a flexible connective tissue made of chondrin that provides cushioning and smooth movement at joints. Comparison: (1) Hardness - Bone is hard due to calcium salts, Cartilage is soft and flexible (2) Blood supply - Bone has rich blood supply, Cartilage has no blood supply (3) Growth - Bone grows throughout life, Cartilage has limited growth capacity
workedExample.markScheme: Definition of bone (1 mark), Definition of cartilage (1 mark), Three valid comparisons (3 marks), Correct technical terminology (1 mark)

examinerWarnings: Students often lose marks by writing general answers instead of textbook-exact definitions. Always use the STBB textbook phrasing for all definitions.
Many students confuse the three types of muscles - remember cardiac muscle is involuntary AND striated, which makes it different from both skeletal and smooth muscle.

When classifying joints, students forget to provide the exact example mentioned in textbook. Freely movable joints example must be 'ball and socket joint found in shoulder', not just 'shoulder joint'.
note_data.quickRecap: Support and movement involves skeletal system (206 bones providing structure), muscular system (skeletal, cardiac, smooth muscles), and joints (immovable, slightly movable, freely movable). Bones are hard connective tissue with calcium phosphate, cartilage is flexible connective tissue with chondrin. Muscles contract to produce movement, with skeletal muscles being voluntary and striated, cardiac muscles involuntary and striated, smooth muscles involuntary and non-striated.
